intro: Caucalis infesta, also known as Black Melanthium or Black Melanthium caucalis, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Apiaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its reported health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Caucalis infesta include alkaloids, saponins, and coumarins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include tea, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat digestive issues, skin problems, and to boost the immune system.
